skill.name: develop
skill.description: Guides AI coding assistants through codebase exploration, implementation planning, targeted edits, refactoring, and verification practices.
skill.summary: Apply structured software development practices for coding, refactoring, and codebase analysis.
skill.author: 0xmsc

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, reconnaissance, and weak cryptography patterns, but review found Markdown documentation rather than executable code. The only retained risk factor is benign external command guidance for local code exploration with tools such as fd and rg.

low_findings.title: Markdown Inline References Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Static matches at these lines are inline Markdown references to a skill, a file, editing tool names, search tools, or Python syntax. They are documentation text, not executable shell commands, and no command interpolation or script execution is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed files contain Markdown guidance only, with no runnable code blocks or scripts. The matched backticks delimit inline text and tool names.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: SKILL.md line 3 is a prose description of codebase analysis and quality improvement. No weak cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, or cryptographic operation appears in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains only descriptive metadata and no crypto-related API call or algorithm name. No supporting evidence for a cryptographic issue was found elsewhere in the two files.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Pattern Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The reported locations describe coding style guidance and targeted file editing. They do not instruct collection of host details, environment secrets, network data, or other reconnaissance output.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is limited to Python import guidance and an edit-file preference. No evidence of system fingerprinting or data collection appears at the cited locations.

low_findings.title: Benign Local Shell Tool Guidance
low_findings.description: NEEDS USER AWARENESS: SKILL.md line 36 recommends fd, rg, and standard shell tools for code exploration and navigation. This is normal developer workflow guidance, but marketplace users should understand that an assistant may run local read-only exploration commands when applying the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line clearly recommends command-line tools, but the intent is local code navigation rather than unsafe execution. Risk depends on how the host assistant requests and enforces command approval.

content.user_title: Improve Code Changes with Structured Workflow
content.value_statement: AI coding assistants can lose context or make broad edits without a clear development process. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through planning, code exploration, focused implementation, and verification.
actual_capabilities: Guides structured planning before non-trivial code changes.
Encourages matching existing code style and project conventions.
Promotes targeted file edits instead of full-file rewrites.
Provides Python guidance for import placement and circular dependency handling.
Includes git safety guidance for branch and repository operations.
limitations: Does not include executable automation scripts or test runners.
Does not provide language-specific guidance beyond a short Python reference.
Does not replace project-specific contribution rules or review requirements.
May require command approval when the host assistant explores files locally.

use_cases.title: Plan a Feature Change
use_cases.description: Use the skill to make an assistant ask clarifying questions, propose a plan, and wait before implementation.

use_cases.title: Refactor Existing Code
use_cases.description: Use the skill to keep refactoring small, style-consistent, and focused on maintainability.

use_cases.title: Review Python Structure
use_cases.description: Use the skill to identify import organization issues and avoid hiding circular dependencies.

prompt_templates.title: Explore Before Editing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the develop skill to inspect this repository, summarize the relevant files, and explain what should be changed before editing anything.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Small Fix
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the develop skill to plan a fix for this issue. Ask clarifying questions with proposed answers, then wait for approval.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the develop skill to refactor this component while preserving behavior, matching local style, and listing verification steps.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate Architecture Impact
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the develop skill to analyze this cross-module change, identify edge cases, propose an implementation sequence, and define tests.

output_examples.input: Plan a change that adds validation to an existing form handler.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the handler, related tests, and validation conventions. It proposes a short plan, asks approval, and lists expected verification.

output_examples.input: Refactor a Python module with circular imports.
output_examples.output: The assistant flags the circular dependency, recommends moving shared definitions, and avoids hiding the problem behind type-checking guards.

output_examples.input: Make a targeted bug fix in an unfamiliar repository.
output_examples.output: The assistant searches for relevant files, edits only the needed area, and reports the verification commands or manual checks used.

best_practices: Provide the repository goal, failing behavior, and any relevant constraints before asking for edits.
Approve the implementation plan only after the assistant identifies affected files and risks.
Ask for verification results after each meaningful code change.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to bypass project contribution or review rules.
Do not request broad rewrites when a targeted edit can solve the issue.
Do not accept silent exception handling for unexpected failures.

faq.question: Does this skill run code by itself?
faq.answer: No. It is Markdown guidance for an AI assistant. Any commands depend on the host tool and its permission model.

faq.question: Which assistants can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it specific to Python?
faq.answer: No. The main skill is general, with one short Python reference for imports and circular dependencies.

faq.question: Why is the risk level low?
faq.answer: The files contain documentation only, but they mention local command-line tools for repository exploration.

faq.question: Can it help with git operations?
faq.answer: Yes. It gives safety guidance to ask before initializing repositories or switching branches.

faq.question: Does it include security scanning?
faq.answer: No. It promotes careful development practices, but it is not a dedicated security scanner.
